title: Wallabag container fails as quadlet but successful with podman run
body:
### Issue Description

This podman command runs successfully

`podman run -p 8181:80 -e "SYMFONY__ENV__DOMAIN_NAME=http://localhost:8181" docker.io/wallabag/wallabag:latest`

However, this quadlet file doesn't work


[Container]
Image=docker.io/wallabag/wallabag:latest
ContainerName=wallabag
PublishPort=8181:80
Environment="SYMFONY__ENV__DOMAIN_NAME=http://localhost:8181"

[Service]
Restart=always



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a container file under ~/.config/containers/systemd/wallabag.container
2. systemctl --user daemon-reload
3. systemctl --user start wallabag
4. Attempt to connect to service at localhost:8181


### Describe the results you received

journalctl reports this error (although the service does not fail)

`Feb 20 02:33:16 fedora wallabag[166752]: nginx: [alert] could not open error log file: open() "/var/lib/nginx/logs/error.log" failed (6: No such device or address)`


### Describe the results you expected

I expected the same results as the podman command.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

I'm running Fedora Silverblue 37

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Could you use quadlet --dryrun on the unit file to see if it shows you an issue?

I get this error when using dryrun

`quadlet-generator[205790]: No files to parse from [/etc/containers/systemd /usr/share/containers/systemd]`

How do I specify the unit/where is it stored by quadlet?

You have to move the quadlet file to one of those directories, if you are running in rootful mode.  If you are running in rootless mode then the file should be moved to $HOME/.config/containers/systemd
Run `/usr/libexec/podman/quadlet -dryrun -user` .   The `-user` flag will look into HOME

Hm, I'm running rootless and the wallabag.container file is in that directory, but no luck. I went ahead and copied it into the /etc location and ran with sudo. Here's the output. 


---wallabag.service---
[X-Container]
Image=docker.io/wallabag/wallabag:latest
ContainerName=wallabag
PublishPort=8181:80
Environment="SYMFONY__ENV__DOMAIN_NAME=http://localhost:8181"

[Service]
Restart=always
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
KillMode=mixed
ExecStopPost=-/usr/bin/podman rm -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
ExecStopPost=-rm -f %t/%N.cid
Delegate=yes
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all
SyslogIdentifier=%N
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name=wallabag --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --log-driver passthrough --runtime /usr/bin/crun --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon -d --publish 8181:80 --env SYMFONY__ENV__DOMAIN_NAME=http://localhost:8181 docker.io/wallabag/wallabag:latest

[Unit]
SourcePath=/etc/containers/systemd/wallabag.container
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

The reason quadlet is failing is it does not see the unit file. In the example above, if you start the service, does it run?


Here is what I see in the homedir.

Here is what I see when I run in rootless mode.

$ /usr/libexec/podman/quadlet --user --dryrun
quadlet-generator[280323]: Loading source unit file /home/dwalsh/.config/containers/systemd/mysleep.container
...

I think this could be caused by `--log-driver passthrough`, maybe try changing it to journald.
Also could you check for selinux errors? `sudo ausearch -m avc -ts recent`
I know the nginx images typically link `/var/lib/nginx/logs/error.log` to `/dev/stderr` so it tries to open stderr again and this fails for some reason.
Adding this line `PodmanArgs=--log-driver journald` to my quadlet file fixed the problem. thanks!

@rhatdan @alexlarsson @ygalblum PTAL

Should we change the default log-driver of Quadlet?  Currently, it's setting `passthrough` unless the user explicitly sets a one in the `.container` file. I have growing feeling that Quadlet should probably just use the system defaults which can be set in `containers.conf`.  Alternatively, a `log-driver-quadlet` option could be added to `containers.conf`.  Some global setting somewhere.
I would prefer to document this and keep it as passthrough.  Having it easy to set LogDriver fixes the issue, and most users will not care.
